model_type: LightGBM multiclass classifier with safe optional sigmoid calibration

target.interpretation: P(Severity=k | Accident, Context). This is conditional on an accident happening.

reasons: Rejected calibration: calibrated model appears collapsed.
Rejected calibration: macro_f1 dropped by more than 0.02.

fixes_applied: Representative full-period sampling for train/validation/calibration/test instead of stopping at early chunks.
Soft square-root inverse-frequency class weighting by default.
Calibration sample cap to reduce runtime.
Automatic calibrated-model rejection if predictions collapse toward one class.
Final model selected using validation diagnostics.
Danger thresholds computed from selected final model, not blindly from calibrated predictions.
Added severe binary metrics for Danger% and ordinal metrics for ordered severity classes.
limitations: This model predicts severity conditional on accident occurrence; it does not predict whether an accident will occur.
The dataset is from the United States, so deployment in Algeria may suffer from domain shift.
Severity classes are highly imbalanced, especially Severity 1 and Severity 4.
Road segment history, speed limits, lane counts, curvature, and traffic volume are not included in this dataset version.
SHAP explanations are model explanations, not causal explanations.
